intro: Alsine facchinii, also known as the Alpine Penny-cress or Italian cress, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Alsine facchinii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Alsine facchinii is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in topical preparations for skin conditions and wounds.
